mission.title: Install and Configure k0s (Zero Friction Kubernetes)
mission.description: k0s is a lightweight, all-inclusive Kubernetes distribution designed for ease of use and quick deployment. It is suitable for various environments including cloud, bare metal, and edge computing. The key trade-off is its simplicity, which may limit advanced customization options compared to other distributions.

steps.title: Install k0s Binary
steps.description:
This step installs the k0s binary on your system, which is required to create and manage your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

curl -sSL https://github.com/k0sproject/k0s/releases/download/v1.35.1+k0s.0/k0s-v1.35.1+k0s.0-linux-amd64 -o /usr/local/bin/k0s

chmod +x /usr/local/bin/k0s

```

> Download the k0s binary
> Make the k0s binary executable

steps.title: Initialize the k0s Controller
steps.description:
This command initializes the k0s controller, which is the master node of your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

k0s init --enable-worker

```

> Initialize the controller and enable worker nodes

steps.title: Start the k0s Controller
steps.description:
This step starts the k0s controller, allowing it to manage the Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

k0s start

```

> Start the k0s controller

steps.title: Configure kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command sets up the kubeconfig file, allowing you to interact with your Kubernetes cluster using kubectl.

```bash

k0s kubeconfig admin > ~/.kube/config

```

> Generate kubeconfig for admin access

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> Verify that the nodes are in 'Ready' state

uninstall.title: Uninstall k0s
uninstall.description:
This step removes the k0s installation and all associated resources. Be aware that this will lead to data loss for any workloads running in the cluster.

```bash

k0s stop

k0s reset

rm /usr/local/bin/k0s

```

> Stop the k0s controller
> Remove kubeconfig file
> Remove the k0s binary

upgrade.title: Upgrade k0s
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your cluster configuration and workloads. This step will upgrade your k0s installation to the latest version.

```bash

k0s stop

curl -sSL https://github.com/k0sproject/k0s/releases/download/v1.35.1+k0s.0/k0s-v1.35.1+k0s.0-linux-amd64 -o /usr/local/bin/k0s

chmod +x /usr/local/bin/k0s

k0s start

kubectl get nodes

```

> Stop the k0s controller before upgrading
> Download the latest k0s binary
> Make the new k0s binary executable
> Start the k0s controller after the upgrade
> Verify that the nodes are in 'Ready' state after the upgrade

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'k0s: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The k0s binary is not installed or not in the system PATH.

**Fix:**
Ensure that k0s is installed and the binary is in /usr/local/bin.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubeconfig not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig file has not been generated or is in the wrong location.

**Fix:**
Run 'k0s kubeconfig admin > ~/.kube/config' to generate the kubeconfig file.

troubleshooting.title: Nodes are in 'NotReady' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The k0s controller may not be running or there may be network issues.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the k0s controller with 'k0s status' and ensure network connectivity.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'k0s init failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient permissions or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Ensure you are running the command with sufficient privileges (e.g., as root) and that all dependencies are met.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for multi-node installations and improved CLI commands.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for upgrading from v1.30.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get nodes' showing all nodes in 'Ready' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources available before installation.
